config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar hanya demi alasan kepentingan masyarakat, karena tanpa perlindungan hak pribadi, mayoritas bisa dengan mudah menindas minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil dan proporsional, bukan dengan mengorbankan kebebasan dasar orang per orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati, karena dari situ muncul kepercayaan, tanggung jawab, dan partisipasi yang lebih kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From a United States perspective, I am very wary of any argument that gives "society" too much power, because that language has often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and abuses against unpopular minorities. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case, since public safety and basic order do matter, but the burden should be on society to justify any limit very carefully. In my view, a healthy society protects individual rights first and then narrowly limits them only when there is a clear, necessary reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis ditempatkan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membungkam kelompok kecil, padahal justru minoritas paling rentan dirugikan kalau hak pribadi dianggap bisa ditawar. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, aturan yang adil, dan alasan yang benar-benar jelas, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa paling benar. Menurut saya, masyarakat sehat bukan yang paling patuh menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi warga satu per satu tanpa mengorbankan kebebasan dasarnya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be treated as automatically more important than the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, I am especially suspicious of vague appeals to "the common good" because they can be used to justify censorship, forced conformity, and mistreatment of unpopular groups. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are limitless; public safety, disease control, and basic order can justify some restrictions, but only when they are narrowly tailored and clearly necessary. To me, a good society is one that starts by protecting individual rights and only limits them in rare cases with strong evidence.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak klaim bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil, padahal justru di situlah risiko penyalahgunaan kekuasaan paling besar. Memang ada situasi seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan alasan kabur untuk memaksa semua orang seragam. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling keras menuntut individu mengalah, melainkan yang paling disiplin melindungi hak dasar setiap warganya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. In the United States, I have seen how broad claims about the "public good" can slide into censorship, discrimination, and pressure to conform, especially against unpopular minorities. At the same time, I do not treat individual rights as unlimited, because things like public health and safety can justify narrow restrictions when there is a real necessity. The key difference is that society should have to prove the need for limits, not assume it is automatically entitled to override personal rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
